channel.title: Posts on My Learning Reflection Journal
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on My Learning Reflection Journal

item.title: Unit 2: Reflections on Information Organization
item.description:
In unit 2 i learned how every file contains information which is hidden the digital structure goes unnoticed. then there is types of data managing types one works with users way and other with the platform builders way which is interesting because it somehow becomes the deciding factor for the user friendly ways.

overall it was fun learning i knew about the hidden files but i know how they work and why they exist

item.title: About Me
item.description:
Hello! I’m Nowman

I’m a student at Azim Premji University studying Information Science. This blog is my reflection journal for documenting my learning journey.

About Me

Name: Nowman Ahamed

Course: Information Science

University: Azim Premji University

Student ID: BSIS1250034

GitHub: nowman-ug25 (https://github.com/nowman-ug25)

My Academic Interests

Information systems and databases

Web development and programming

Data analysis and visualization

Digital literacy and ethics

Purpose of This Blog This space will serve as:

item.title: My First Reflection
item.description:
INTRODUCTION

Information Science sits at the fascinating intersection of people, technology, and data. As I begin this course, I see it as the study of how information is created, organized, managed, and used as discipline that shapes everything from library databases to the algorithms that power our daily digital interactions. This journal will track my journey into understanding these complex systems.

item.title: Unit 1: First Steps into Information Science
item.description:
Introduction

Information Science sits at the fascinating intersection of people, technology, and data. As I begin this course, I see it as the study of how information is created, organized, managed, and used—a discipline that shapes everything from library databases to the algorithms that power our daily digital interactions. This journal will track my journey into understanding these complex systems.
